q: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>4</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> mixture of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>NaOH</mi><mo>,</mo><msub><mi>Na</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi>CO</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> and some inert impurities was first titrated with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mn>10</mn></mfrac><mi>HCl</mi></math> using phenolphthalein as an indicator, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>17</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>HCl</mi></math> was required at the end point. After this methyl orange was added and titrated. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of same <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>HCl</mi></math> was required for the next end point. The weight percentage of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>Na</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi>CO</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> in the mixture is (Rounded-off to the nearest integer)
question: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>4</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> mixture of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>NaOH</mi><mo>,</mo><msub><mi>Na</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi>CO</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> and some inert impurities was first titrated with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mn>10</mn></mfrac><mi>HCl</mi></math> using phenolphthalein as an indicator, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>17</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>HCl</mi></math> was required at the end point. After this methyl orange was added and titrated. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of same <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>HCl</mi></math> was required for the next end point. The weight percentage of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>Na</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi>CO</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> in the mixture is (Rounded-off to the nearest integer)
solution:
Upto first end point gm equi. of NaOH+Na2CO3=HCl

x+y×1=110×17.5

x+y=1.75   ....1

Upto second end point

NaOH+Na2CO3≡HCl

x+y×2=110×19

x+2 y=1.9   ....2

y=0.15

%Na2CO3=0.15×10-3×1060.4×100

=3.975%

=4%

Hence answer is 4

subject: Chemistry
q:
Which of the following options are correct for the reaction?

2Au(CN)2-(aq)+Zn(s)→2Au(s)+Zn(CN)42-(aq)

A. Redox reaction

B. Displacement reaction

C. Decomposition reaction

D. Combination reaction

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
question:
Which of the following options are correct for the reaction?

2Au(CN)2-(aq)+Zn(s)→2Au(s)+Zn(CN)42-(aq)

A. Redox reaction

B. Displacement reaction

C. Decomposition reaction

D. Combination reaction

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
solution:
Gold is then extracted from this complex by displacement technique by using a more electropositive zinc metal.

In this method, zinc (Zn) acts as a reducing agent and it reduces Au + to Au and itself gets oxidized from Zn to Zn+2 ions which combine with CN- ions and form soluble complex, sodium tetracyanozincate (II).

2Au(CN)2- aq+Zn s→2Au s+Zn(CN)42- aq

q:

Assertion A :Phenolphthalein is a pH dependent indicator, remains colourless in acidic solution and gives pink colour in basic medium

Reason R : Phenolphthalein is a weak acid. It doesn't dissociate in basic medium.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below
question:
Given below are two statements: one is labelled as Assertion A and the other is labelled as Reason R.

Assertion A :Phenolphthalein is a pH dependent indicator, remains colourless in acidic solution and gives pink colour in basic medium

Reason R : Phenolphthalein is a weak acid. It doesn't dissociate in basic medium.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below
options: Both <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> are true and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> is the correct explanation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math>

Both <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> are true but <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> is NOT the correct explanation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math>.
<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> is true but <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> is false
<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> is false but <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> is true
solution:
Phenolphthalein, (C20H14O4), an organic compound of the phthalein family that is widely employed as an acid-base indicator. As an indicator of a solution’s pH, phenolphthalein is colourless below pH 8.5 and attains a pink to deep red hue above pH 9.0.

Phenolphthalein dissociate in basic medium

HPhaqcolourless⇌H++Ph-Pink

Oxidizing power of the species will increase in the order:
question:
Given:
Co3++e-→Co2+;E°=+1.81V
Pb3++2e-→Pb2+;E°=+1.67V

solution:
Higher the value of ERed0; higher will be its oxidizing power.

Hence correct order is Bi+3<Ce+4<Pb+4<Co+3

subject: Chemistry
q:
Which of the following reactions are disproportionation reactions?

1 Cu+→Cu2++Cu

2 3MnO42-+4H+→2MnO4-+MnO2+2H2O

3 2KMnO4→K2MnO4+MnO2+O2

4 2MnO4-+3Mn2++2H2O→5MnO2+4H+

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
question:
Which of the following reactions are disproportionation reactions?

1 Cu+→Cu2++Cu

2 3MnO42-+4H+→2MnO4-+MnO2+2H2O

3 2KMnO4→K2MnO4+MnO2+O2

4 2MnO4-+3Mn2++2H2O→5MnO2+4H+

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
solution:
In disproportionation reaction,  both oxidation and reduction of same chemical species occurs.

  • Oxidation is gain in oxidation number.
  • Reduction is loss in oxidation number.

1) Cu+⟶Cu2++Cu

Oxidation state of Cu+=+1.

Oxidation state of Cu2+=+2.

Oxidation state of Cu=0 .

Here, Cu+ is getting both reduced and oxidised.

2) 3MnO4-+4H+⟶2MnO4-+MnO2+2H2O

Oxidation state of Mn in MnO4-=+7.

Oxidation state of Mn in MnO2 =+4.

Here, reduction is taking place.

3) 2KMnO4⟶K2MnO4+MnO2+O2

Here in the reaction, Mn is changing its oxidation state from +7 to +6 and +4. In both cases, reduction is occurring.

4) 2MnO4-+3Mn2++2H2O⟶5MnO2+4H+

Here in the reaction, product with Mn in only one, so there is no possibility of both oxidation and reduction reactions at the same time.

q:

H 2 SO 3 aq + Sn aq 4 + + H 2 O l⁡ → Sn aq 2 + + HSO 4 aq - + 3 H aq +

options: Sn<sup>4</sup><sup>+</sup> is the oxidizing agent because it undergoes oxidation

H<sub>2</sub>SO<sub>3</sub> is the reducing agent because it undergoes reduction
H<sub>2</sub>SO<sub>3</sub> is the reducing agent because it undergoes oxidation
solution:
Identify oxidation states of all elements in Redox Reaction

H2SO+43+Sn4++H2O(l)→Sn2++HSO4- +6 +3H+

S is changing the oxidation state from (+4) to (+6). i.e H2SO3 is a Reducing agent because it undergoes oxidation.

<math><mtable><mtr><mtd><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi></mtd><mtd><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">d</mi></mtd><mtd><mi mathvariant="normal">E</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">d</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi></mtd></mtr><mtr><mtd><mi mathvariant="normal">W</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi></mtd><mtd><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">r</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></mtd><mtd><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">u</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">r</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi></mtd></mtr></mtable></math>

<math><mtable><mtr><mtd><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi></mtd><mtd><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">d</mi></mtd><mtd><mi mathvariant="normal">E</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">d</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi></mtd></mtr><mtr><mtd><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">r</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></mtd><mtd><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">r</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></mtd><mtd><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">h</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">r</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">d</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">w</mi></mtd></mtr></mtable></math>

<math><mtable><mtr><mtd><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi></mtd><mtd><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">d</mi></mtd><mtd><mi mathvariant="normal">E</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">d</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi></mtd></mtr><mtr><mtd><mi mathvariant="normal">W</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi></mtd><mtd><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">r</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></mtd><mtd><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">w</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">h</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">r</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">d</mi></mtd></mtr></mtable></math>
solution:
The pH range between 3.1 (red) and 4.4 (yellow) is the colour-change interval of methyl orange.

Weak base has pH > 7. So, when methyl orange indicator is added the solution turns yellow & if the solution is titrated against strong acid it turns pinkish red at the end point i.e., pH <3.1.

subject: Chemistry
q: In order to oxidise a mixture of $1$ mole each of $FeC_2O_4$, $Fe_2(C_2O_4)_3$, $FeSO_4$ and $Fe_2(SO_4)_3$ in acidic medium, the number of moles of $KMnO_4$ required is
question: In order to oxidise a mixture of $1$ mole each of $FeC_2O_4$, $Fe_2(C_2O_4)_3$, $FeSO_4$ and $Fe_2(SO_4)_3$ in acidic medium, the number of moles of $KMnO_4$ required is
solution:
In acidic medium, $KMnO_4$ acts as an oxidising agent and gets reduced to $Mn^{2+}$. The n-factor for $KMnO_4$ is $5$.

The n-factors for the given reducing agents are calculated based on the total number of moles of electrons lost per mole of the compound:

For $FeC_2O_4$:
$Fe^{2+} \rightarrow Fe^{3+} + e^{-}$
$C_2O_4^{2-} \rightarrow 2CO_2 + 2e^{-}$
Total electrons lost $= 3$, so n-factor $= 3$.
Equivalents $= 1 \times 3 = 3$.

For $Fe_2(C_2O_4)_3$:
$Fe^{3+}$ is not oxidised.
$3C_2O_4^{2-} \rightarrow 6CO_2 + 6e^{-}$
Total electrons lost $= 6$, so n-factor $= 6$.
Equivalents $= 1 \times 6 = 6$.

For $FeSO_4$:
$Fe^{2+} \rightarrow Fe^{3+} + e^{-}$
$SO_4^{2-}$ is not oxidised.
Total electrons lost $= 1$, so n-factor $= 1$.
Equivalents $= 1 \times 1 = 1$.

For $Fe_2(SO_4)_3$:
Neither $Fe^{3+}$ nor $SO_4^{2-}$ can be oxidised further.
n-factor $= 0$.
Equivalents $= 0$.

Total equivalents of the reducing mixture $= 3 + 6 + 1 + 0 = 10$.

Let the number of moles of $KMnO_4$ required be $x$.
Equivalents of $KMnO_4 = x \times 5$.

Equating the equivalents of the oxidising and reducing agents:
$5x = 10$
$x = 2$

Answer: $2$

subject: Chemistry
q: KMnO4 is titrated with ferrous ammonium sulphate hexahydrate in presence of dilute H2SO4. Number of water molecules produced for 2 molecules of KMnO4 is _______
question: KMnO4 is titrated with ferrous ammonium sulphate hexahydrate in presence of dilute H2SO4. Number of water molecules produced for 2 molecules of KMnO4 is _______
solution:
By balancing redox reaction:

10[FeSO4.(NH4)2SO4.6H2O]+2KMNO4+8H2SO4→5Fe2(SO4)3+2MnSO4+10(NH4)2SO4+K2SO4+68H2O

8H++MnO4-+5Fe+2⟶5Fe+3+Mn+2+4H2O

For 2 moles of MnO4-, 8 moles of H2O. are released 5 moles of Mohr's salt contain 30 moles of H2O. So, 10 moles of Mohr's salt contain 60 moles of H2O So, total moles of H2O produced=68

subject: Chemistry
q:
The neutralization occurs when 10 mL of 0.1 M acid 'A' is allowed to react with 30 mL of 0.05 M base MOH2. The basicity of the acid 'A' is
[M is a metal]
question:
The neutralization occurs when 10 mL of 0.1 M acid 'A' is allowed to react with 30 mL of 0.05 M base MOH2. The basicity of the acid 'A' is
[M is a metal]
solution:
Let basicity of acid is $x$

A+MOH2⟶Salt+Water

At complete neturization.

Milli eq. of acid = Milli eq. of base

n0.1×10=20.05×30

n=3

question: In order to oxidize a mixture of one mole of each of <math><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow></msub><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><msub><mrow><mo>(</mo><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow></msub><mo>)</mo></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow></msub></math> and <math><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><msub><mrow><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow></msub><mo>)</mo></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub></math> in acidic medium, the number of moles of <math><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow></msub></math> is:
solution:
Eq.KMnO4⇒Eq.FeSO4+Eq.Fe2SO43+EqFeC2O4+Eq.Fe2C2O43
KMnO4⇒MnO4-
5e-+8H++MnO4-⇒Mn+2+4H2O V.F=5

So Eq.KMnO4⇒Eq.FeSO4+Eq.FeC2O4+Eq.Fe2C2O43
n×5=1×1+1×0+1×3+1×6
n×5=1+3+6
n=105=2 mole

question:
In acidic medium, K2Cr2O7 shows oxidising action as represented in the half reaction

Cr2O72-+XH++Ye-→2 A+ZH2O X, Y, Z and A are respectively are:
solution:
Potassium dichromate, K2Cr2O7, is a common inorganic chemical reagent, most commonly used as an oxidizing agent in various laboratory and industrial applications. 

The balanced reaction is,

Cr2O72-+14H++6e-→2Cr3++7H2O

X=14

Y=6

A=7

subject: Chemistry
q:
Given below are two statements:
Statement I: Sodium dichromate and potassium dichromate are classified as primary standards in titrimetric analysis.
Statement II: Phenolphthalein is a weak base, therefore it dissociates in acidic medium.
In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below
question:
Given below are two statements:
Statement I: Sodium dichromate and potassium dichromate are classified as primary standards in titrimetric analysis.
Statement II: Phenolphthalein is a weak base, therefore it dissociates in acidic medium.
In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are true
Both Statement I and Statement II are false
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Statement I is false but Statement II is true
solution:
Statement I is false because sodium dichromate is deliquescent (absorbs moisture from the atmosphere) and cannot be weighed accurately, so it is not used as a primary standard. Potassium dichromate, however, is a primary standard.

Statement II is false because phenolphthalein is a weak organic acid, not a weak base. It remains largely undissociated in an acidic medium (colorless) and dissociates in a basic medium to give a pink color.

Therefore, both Statement I and Statement II are false.

Answer: Both Statement I and Statement II are false

subject: Chemistry
q: A compound '<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math>' is a weak acid and it exhibits colour change at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>pH</mi></math> close to the equivalence point during neutralization of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>NaOH</mi></math> with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>CH</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mi>COOH</mi></math>. Compound '<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math>' exists in ionized form in basic medium. The compound '<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math>' is
question: A compound '<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math>' is a weak acid and it exhibits colour change at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>pH</mi></math> close to the equivalence point during neutralization of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>NaOH</mi></math> with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>CH</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mi>COOH</mi></math>. Compound '<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math>' exists in ionized form in basic medium. The compound '<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math>' is
solution:
phenolphthalein, (C20H14O4), an organic compound of the phthalein family that is widely employed as an acid-base indicator. As an indicator of a solution's pH, phenolphthalein is colourless below pH 8.5 and attains a pink to deep red hue above pH 9.0.

It is used for the titration of weak acid and strong base. Phenolphthalein is weak acid give colour in basic medium. It exist in ionized form in basic medium.

subject: Chemistry
q: Which of the following cannot function as an oxidising agent?
question: Which of the following cannot function as an oxidising agent?
solution:
An oxidising agent is a substance that oxidises other substances involved in the reaction by gaining or accepting electrons from them.

So the lowest oxidation state substance will go to the higher oxidation state, which means it reduces others. Hence, it acts as a reducing agent. -3 is the lowest oxidation state for nitrogen. Hence, it only acts as a reducing agent.

Hence option A is the answer.

during the reaction, oxidation of nitrogen take place from 0 to 2 and reduction of oxygen take place from 0 to –2. It means this reaction is redox reaction.

q:
Given below are two statements:
Statement I: In the oxalic acid vs $\mathrm{KMnO}_4$ (in the presence of dil $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ ) titration the solution needs to be heated initially to $60^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$, but no heating is required in Ferrous ammonium sulphate (FAS) vs $\mathrm{KMnO}_4$ titration (in the presence of dil $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ )
Statement II: In oxalic acid vs $\mathrm{KMnO}_4$ titration, the initial formation of $\mathrm{MnSO}_4$ takes place at high temperature, which then acts as catalyst for further reaction. In the case of FAS vs $\mathrm{KMnO}_4$, heating oxidizes $\mathrm{Fe}^{2+}$ into $\mathrm{Fe}^{3+}$ by oxygen of air and error may be introduced in the experiment.
In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below
question:
Given below are two statements:
Statement I: In the oxalic acid vs $\mathrm{KMnO}_4$ (in the presence of dil $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ ) titration the solution needs to be heated initially to $60^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$, but no heating is required in Ferrous ammonium sulphate (FAS) vs $\mathrm{KMnO}_4$ titration (in the presence of dil $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ )
Statement II: In oxalic acid vs $\mathrm{KMnO}_4$ titration, the initial formation of $\mathrm{MnSO}_4$ takes place at high temperature, which then acts as catalyst for further reaction. In the case of FAS vs $\mathrm{KMnO}_4$, heating oxidizes $\mathrm{Fe}^{2+}$ into $\mathrm{Fe}^{3+}$ by oxygen of air and error may be introduced in the experiment.
In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are false
Both Statement I and Statement II are true
Statement I is false but Statement II is true
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
solution:
Heating is required in oxalic acid filtration due to High activation energy.

Heating is not required in FAS vs $\mathrm{KMnO}_4$ titration because $\mathrm{Fe}^{2+}$ will get converted into $\mathrm{Fe}^{3+}$ by oxygen of air and error may be introduced in the experiment.
Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct.

subject: Chemistry
q: The compound that cannot act both as oxidizing and reducing agent is
question: The compound that cannot act both as oxidizing and reducing agent is
solution:
In HNO2, Nitrogen has oxidation state +3, nitrogen has maximum oxidation state +5 and minimum -3, hence nitrous acid can act both as oxidizing and reducing agent.

Sulphur has maximum oxidation state +6 and minimum is -2, in H2SO3 sulphur has oxidation state +4, hence it can act as both oxidizing and reducing agent.

For oxygen maximum oxidation state is +2 and minimum is -2, in H2O2 Oxygen has oxidation state -1, hence it can act bot as oxidizing and reducing agent.

As in H3PO4, Phosphorous is present in it’s maximum oxidation state i.e., +5, hence it cannot act as reducing agent.

+7MnO4−  +  −1H2O2vf=2   →   +2Mn2+ +  0O2

x342=0.316158 5

So, % purity of H2O2 solution =0.170.2×100=85%

subject: Chemistry
q: One mole of $\mathrm{Cl}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})$ was passed into 2 L of cold 2 M KOH solution. After the reaction, the concentrations of $\mathrm{Cl}^{-}, \mathrm{ClO}^{-}$and $\mathrm{OH}^{-}$are respectively (assume volume remains constant)
question: One mole of $\mathrm{Cl}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})$ was passed into 2 L of cold 2 M KOH solution. After the reaction, the concentrations of $\mathrm{Cl}^{-}, \mathrm{ClO}^{-}$and $\mathrm{OH}^{-}$are respectively (assume volume remains constant)
solution:
Cl₂ undergoes disproportionation in cold KOH: $Cl_2 + 2OH^- \rightarrow Cl^- + ClO^- + H_2O$
Initial: 1 mole Cl₂, 2 L × 2 M KOH = 4 moles OH⁻
Molar ratio Cl₂:OH⁻ = 1:2 (stoichiometric)
Products formed: 1 mole Cl⁻, 1 mole ClO⁻; Excess OH⁻ = 4 - 2 = 2 moles
In 2 L solution: [Cl⁻] = 0.5 M, [ClO⁻] = 0.5 M, [OH⁻] = 1 M

question:
Consider the following reactions:
NaCl+K2Cr2O7+H2SO4Conc.→A+ side products
A+NaOH→B+ side products
B+H2SO4dilute+H2O2→C+ side products
The sum of the total number of atoms in one molecule each of A,B and C is ________
solution:
A=CrO2Cl2
B=Na2CrO4
C=CrO5

Chromyl chloride CrO2Cl2 can be prepared by the reaction of potassium chromate or potassium dichromate with hydrogen chloride in the presence of sulfuric acid, followed by distillation. The sulfuric acid serves as the dehydration agent.

q:
See the following chemical reaction:

Cr2O72-+XH++6Fe2+→YCr3++6Fe3++ZH2O

The sum of X, Y and Z is
question:
See the following chemical reaction:

Cr2O72-+XH++6Fe2+→YCr3++6Fe3++ZH2O

The sum of X, Y and Z is
solution:
The given equation can be balanced using ion-electron method as shown below.

Fe2+→Fe3++e-         -oxidation Half reaction

6e-+14H++Cr2O72-→2Cr3++7H2O -Reduction Half reaction

The overall reaction is 

6Fe2++14H++Cr2O72-→6Fe3++2Cr3++7H2O

The values X=14,Y=2 and Z = 7

(X+ Y +Z)  = 14 + 2 +7

= 23

subject: Chemistry
q:
How many electrons are involved in the following redox reaction ?

    Cr 2 O 7 2 - + Fe 2 + + C 2 O 4 2 - → Cr 3 + + Fe 3 + + CO 2  (Unbalanced)
question:
How many electrons are involved in the following redox reaction ?

    Cr 2 O 7 2 - + Fe 2 + + C 2 O 4 2 - → Cr 3 + + Fe 3 + + CO 2  (Unbalanced)
solution:
[F e 2+ + C 2 O 4 2−   →  F e 3+ +2C O 2 +3 e − ]×2(oxidation)
C r 2 O 7 2− +14 H + +6 e −   →  2C r 3+ +7 H 2 O(reduction)
_____________________________________________________

Cr2O72−+2Fe 2++2C2O42−+14H+→2Cr3++2Fe3++4CO2+7H2O

Hence, total 6 electrons are involved in the net redox reaction to happen.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Compounds that should not be used as primary standards in titrimetric analysis are :
A. $\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7$
B. Oxalic acid
C. NaOH
D. $\mathrm{FeSO}_4 \cdot 6 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$
E. Sodium tetraborate
Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below:
question:
Compounds that should not be used as primary standards in titrimetric analysis are :
A. $\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7$
B. Oxalic acid
C. NaOH
D. $\mathrm{FeSO}_4 \cdot 6 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$
E. Sodium tetraborate
Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below:
solution:
The primary standard is a highly pure stable compound with a known exact composition that can be accurately weighed and dissolved to creat a solution of known concentration.
NaOH is hygroscopic and can't be used.
$\mathrm{FeSO}_4 \cdot 6 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ is unstable and can be easily oxidised.
$\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7$ is hygroscopic and can't be used.

subject: Chemistry
q: Choose the correct representation of conductometric titration of benzoic acid vs sodium hydroxide.
question: Choose the correct representation of conductometric titration of benzoic acid vs sodium hydroxide.
solution:
C6H5CWAOOH+NaOSBH→C6H5CSaltOONa+H2O

 Initially the conductance is low due to the feeble ionisation of acetic acid. On the addition of base, there is decrease in conductance(A→B) not only due to the replacement of H+ by Na+ but also suppresses the dissociation of benzoic acid due to common ion benzoate. But very soon, the conductance increases(B→C) on adding NaOH as NaOH neutralises the un-dissociated C6H5COOH to C6H5COONa which is the strong electrolyte. This increase in conductance continues raise up to the equivalence point. The graph near the equivalence point is curved due the hydrolysis of salt C6H5COONa. Beyond the equivalence point, conductance increases(C→D) more rapidly with the addition of NaOH due to the highly conducting OH− ions

q: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of aqueous solution of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Fe</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> in acidic medium completely reacted with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>20</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>03</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi></math> aqueous <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>Cr</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msup><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>7</mn></msub><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></msup><mo>.</mo></math> The molarity of the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Fe</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> solution is _______ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi></math> (Round off to the Nearest Integer).

neqFe2+=neqCr2O72-

subject: Chemistry
q:
Chlorine undergoes disproportionation in alkaline medium as shown below :
aCl2( g)+bOH-(aq)→cClO-(aq)+dCl-(aq)+eH2O(l)

The values of a, b, c and d in a balanced redox reaction are respectively :
question:
Chlorine undergoes disproportionation in alkaline medium as shown below :
aCl2( g)+bOH-(aq)→cClO-(aq)+dCl-(aq)+eH2O(l)

The values of a, b, c and d in a balanced redox reaction are respectively :
solution:
Chlorine undergoes disproportionation reaction in alkaline medium. It is the simultaneous oxidation-reduction. Chlorine is simultaneously reduced to chloride ion Cl− and is oxidised to  ClO−  ion. Halogens (e.g. Cl) have a strong tendency to accept electrons, so they act as strong oxidising agents.

The reaction is as follows,

\(\Rightarrow \mathrm{Cl}_2 - 2 \mathrm{O} \mathrm{H}^- \longrightarrow \mathrm{Cl}^{-}+\mathrm{ClO}^{-}+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\)

Hence the values of a,b,c,d are 1,2,1,1.

subject: Chemistry
q: Manganese <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi>VI</mi></mfenced></math> has ability to disproportionate in acidic solution. The difference in oxidation states of two ions it forms in acidic solution is
question: Manganese <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi>VI</mi></mfenced></math> has ability to disproportionate in acidic solution. The difference in oxidation states of two ions it forms in acidic solution is
solution:
In acidic solution Mn(VI) become unstable relative to Mn(VII) and Mn(IV)

3MnO42-+4H+→2MnO4-+MnO2+2H2O
So difference in oxidation state of product ions of Mn is = 3

subject: Chemistry
q:
The reaction of sulphur in alkaline medium is given below:

S8s+a OHaq−→b Saq2−+c S2O3aq2−+d H2Ol

The values of 'a' is______ (Integer answer)
question:
The reaction of sulphur in alkaline medium is given below:

S8s+a OHaq−→b Saq2−+c S2O3aq2−+d H2Ol

The values of 'a' is______ (Integer answer)
solution:
For balancing in basic medium add equal number of OH⊖. That of H+

2 S8+12H2O+24OH⊖⟶8 S2-+4 S2O82-+24H2O

2 S8+24OH⊖→8 S2-+4 S2O82-+12H2O

S8+12OH⊖→4 S2-+2 S2O82-+6H2O

a=12

subject: Chemistry
q: In basic medium <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msubsup><mi>CrO</mi><mn>4</mn><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></msubsup></math> oxidises <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msubsup><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>3</mn><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></msubsup></math> to form <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><msub><mi>SO</mi><mn>4</mn></msub><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></msup></math> and itself changes into <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Cr</mi><msubsup><mfenced><mi>OH</mi></mfenced><mn>4</mn><mo>-</mo></msubsup></math>.The volume of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>154</mn><msubsup><mi>MCrO</mi><mn>4</mn><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></msubsup></math> required to react with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>40</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>25</mn><msub><mi>MS</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msup><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></msup></math> is _____ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>mL</mi><mo>.</mo></math> (Rounded-off to the nearest integer)
question: In basic medium <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msubsup><mi>CrO</mi><mn>4</mn><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></msubsup></math> oxidises <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msubsup><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>3</mn><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></msubsup></math> to form <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><msub><mi>SO</mi><mn>4</mn></msub><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></msup></math> and itself changes into <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Cr</mi><msubsup><mfenced><mi>OH</mi></mfenced><mn>4</mn><mo>-</mo></msubsup></math>.The volume of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>154</mn><msubsup><mi>MCrO</mi><mn>4</mn><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></msubsup></math> required to react with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>40</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>25</mn><msub><mi>MS</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msup><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></msup></math> is _____ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>mL</mi><mo>.</mo></math> (Rounded-off to the nearest integer)
solution:
CrO42-+S2O32-→SO42-+CrOH4+3

gm equi. of CrO42-=S2O32-

0.154×3×v=0.25×40×8

v=173.16=173ml

Hence answer is 173
